authoritative_sources.title: RFC 4271: A Border Gateway Protocol 4 (BGP-4)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Standards-track BGP-4 reference for route exchange, AS path behavior, path selection attributes, policy decisions, route aggregation, and loop prevention.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: BGP topology review, neighbor state evidence, route-table interpretation, route-policy decision boundary

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 7454 / BCP 194: BGP Operations and Security
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Best Current Practice for operational BGP security, including prefix filtering, max-prefix filtering, AS-path filtering, control-plane protection, session protection, and community handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: route-policy inspection, edge hardening review, change safety, incident prevention
practical_use_in_pack: prefix and AS path filtering requirements

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 8212: Default External BGP Route Propagation Behavior without Policies
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF standard that changes safe default eBGP route propagation expectations when explicit import or export policies are absent.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: policy prerequisite review, hold/release recommendation, route advertisement safety

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 9234: Route Leak Prevention and Detection Using Roles in UPDATE and OPEN Messages
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF standard for BGP relationship roles and only-to-customer route leak marking and detection.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: route-leak prevention, peer relationship classification, repeat defect escalation

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 6811: BGP Prefix Origin Validation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF standard for validating BGP route origin AS authorization using RPKI data.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: route authenticity review, prefix ownership evidence, hijack-risk controls

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 7606: Revised Error Handling for BGP UPDATE Messages
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF standard for safer handling of malformed BGP UPDATE messages and related session stability behavior.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: control-plane incident handling, malformed route triage, session stability review

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 8092: BGP Large Communities Attribute
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF standard defining large communities used by modern operators for unambiguous BGP policy signaling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: provider policy interpretation, route community review, route steering evidence

authoritative_sources.title: Autonomous System (AS) Numbers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IANA is the authoritative public registry for ASN allocation ranges and RIR allocation responsibility.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: ASN evidence check, public/private ASN caveat, route ownership prerequisite

authoritative_sources.title: MANRS Actions for Network Operators
authoritative_sources.publisher: Mutually Agreed Norms for Routing Security
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized routing-security operating program defining network-operator actions for filtering, anti-spoofing, coordination, and global validation of routing information.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: routing-security posture review, coordination evidence, route leak prevention, operator contact expectations

authoritative_sources.title: Direct Connect routing policies and BGP communities
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AWS official documentation for Direct Connect routing policies, accepted prefixes, BGP communities, local preference, and active/active or active/passive routing behavior.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: AWS edge interconnect review, community policy, provider-specific route preference

authoritative_sources.title: ExpressRoute routing requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Microsoft official documentation for ExpressRoute BGP peering requirements, route filters, prefix limits, accepted ASNs, and route advertisement behavior.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Azure edge interconnect prerequisites, BGP peering review, route filter and prefix-limit evidence
practical_use_in_pack: source conflict and tenant adaptation scenarios

authoritative_sources.title: Cloud Router overview
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Google Cloud official documentation defining Cloud Router as the managed BGP control plane for Cloud Interconnect, HA VPN, and Router appliance connections.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Google Cloud edge BGP session review, learned and advertised route evidence, platform boundary check

authoritative_sources.title: BGP route policies overview
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Google Cloud official documentation for filtering routes or modifying attributes in Cloud Router BGP route policies.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: provider-specific policy inspection, materialization prerequisites, route filter verification

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5: Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized public control catalog covering boundary protection, configuration management, audit, continuous monitoring, incident response, contingency planning, and evidence expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: assurance review, hold/release recommendation, evidence traceability, incident and corrective-action handling

authoritative_sources.title: Cross-Sector Cybersecurity Performance Goals
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CISA publishes cross-sector cybersecurity guidance for critical infrastructure and enterprise operators, including asset exposure, segmentation, logging, monitoring, and resilience practices.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: cross-industry control objective review, resilience evidence, safety and escalation caveats

authoritative_sources.title: Current package task baseline from internet-edge-and-bgp-specialist
authoritative_sources.source_class: Current package task baseline from internet-edge-and-bgp-specialist
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Repository-owned legacy package baseline preserves prior supported tasks, inputs, outputs, and refusal rules, but is treated only as context because the refreshed spec pack is now canonical.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: legacy boundary preservation, scenario baseline, stale assumption replacement
practical_use_in_pack: minimum scenario baseline retained and exceeded

workflow_stages.name: intake and evidence prerequisite check
workflow_stages.description: Classify scope, requested authority, tenant, affected edge, ASNs, prefixes, provider, systems of record, source freshness, route-policy evidence, telemetry, change context, rollback, approval, hold status, and source precedence before offering path-impacting guidance.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: internet-edge topology and peering review
workflow_stages.description: Inspect eBGP peers, upstreams, IXPs, cloud interconnects, link and site diversity, failover paths, authentication, BFD/timer posture, graceful restart assumptions, traffic classes, and provider-specific requirements.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: route-policy and routing-security control inspection
workflow_stages.description: Review import/export policy, prefix filters, max-prefix, AS_PATH, MED, local preference, communities, default-reject posture, RPKI/ROA and IRR evidence, route-leak controls, and least-privilege route advertisements.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: fault isolation and incident evidence review
workflow_stages.description: Use BGP neighbor state, route churn, session flap history, advertised and received routes, route collectors, looking glasses, logs, telemetry, packet or flow evidence, incident records, and change history to isolate session, policy, reachability, blackhole, or route-leak failures.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: disposition, exception, hold, and release recommendation
workflow_stages.description: Evaluate whether evidence supports release, limited exception, hold, blocked closure, rollback, or more-evidence-needed status; require owner, approval, monitoring, rollback, provider constraints, and acceptance criteria.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: corrective-action escalation and adjacent authority routing
workflow_stages.description: Escalate recurring route leaks, repeated session instability, missing ownership, source conflicts, provider action, safety-control bypasses, and work belonging to endpoint, identity, DNS, load balancing, firewall, application, SOC, legal, procurement, or production execution owners.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Current package task baseline from internet-edge-and-bgp-specialist

workflow_stages.name: operator-owned implementation guidance and materialization package
workflow_stages.description: Translate reviewed findings into bounded architecture notes, route-policy recommendations, validation checklist, evidence links, assumptions, confidence, escalation gates, and rollback criteria suitable for a human operator or future specification-based materializer.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: ASNs, public/private ASN posture, prefix ownership records, RIR allocation references, IRR route objects, ROAs, origin-validation state, and peering or provider authorization records

edge topology diagrams, provider and circuit inventory, IXP and cloud interconnect records, peer inventory, edge router configuration, BGP neighbor summaries, BFD/timer settings, authentication settings, route reflector context, and failover path maps

import and export policies, prefix lists, route maps or policy statements, communities, large communities, AS_PATH controls, MED, local preference, max-prefix, route dampening, default-route policy, and route-leak controls

advertised and received route tables, route collector or looking-glass views, BMP or telemetry exports, session logs, syslog, NMS metrics, flow records, packet summaries, incident tickets, and problem records

change records, maintenance windows, rollback plans, freeze status, acceptance tests, provider maintenance notices, provider ticket references, exception requests, approval records, hold or release notes, and risk register entries
quality or safety review notes, control mappings, incident summaries, corrective-action escalations, assumption logs, confidence statements, evidence gap lists, and adjacent-lane handoff packets
systems_of_record: network source of truth, topology repository, edge design repository, device inventory, CMDB, service catalog, and architecture decision records
IPAM, RIR records, IANA ASN registry context, IRR repositories, RPKI/ROA management records, PeeringDB or NOC contact records, prefix authorization store, and tenant standards library

router configuration repository, network automation platform, BGP policy repository, version-control system, change-management platform, approval matrix, exception register, quality hold tracker, and corrective-action tracker

NMS, route collectors, looking glasses, BMP or streaming telemetry store, syslog, SIEM, flow-log store, packet capture store, monitoring dashboards, alerting system, incident-management system, and problem-management system

provider portals, cloud interconnect records, carrier maintenance notices, provider tickets, risk register, contractual or regulatory evidence archive, and source authority registry represented by manifest.yaml and evaluation/research-summary.json

allowed: classify internet-edge and BGP requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, blocked, or requiring adjacent routing
review and design BGP peering, failover posture, route import/export policy, prefix filtering, route advertisement, RPKI/IRR evidence, route-leak controls, monitoring, and rollback guidance
troubleshoot BGP session, route churn, partial reachability, blackhole, route leak, and provider-path failures with evidence-backed steps
recommend risk-ranked remediation, validation checks, rollout sequence, rollback criteria, limited exception scope, hold/release posture, and corrective-action escalation without final authority
prepare materialization-safe guidance for human operators or future specification-based runtime generation
escalate: critical ASN, prefix ownership, route-policy, topology, telemetry, provider, approval, rollback, safety, source precedence, or business criticality evidence is missing
public guidance, provider documentation, tenant policy, contract terms, or approval authority materially conflict

final approval, release, risk acceptance, legal interpretation, carrier contract decision, procurement, provider ticket execution, customer communication, executive authority, or production execution is required

request crosses into endpoint, identity, DNS/DHCP/IPAM, load balancing, firewall/perimeter, WAN/SD-WAN, VPN, wireless, network monitoring operation, SOC containment, forensics, or application engineering lanes
refuse: perform endpoint management, IAM design, application-only architecture, software remediation, penetration testing, exploitation, forensics, legal attestation, procurement approval, carrier pricing or contracting, provider ticket execution, or direct production BGP changes

push router policy, execute CLI or API changes, clear holds, close findings, approve route advertisements, approve exceptions, or bypass route filters, max-prefix, monitoring, route-leak prevention, safety, quality, or change-control boundaries without authority
provide DNS-only, DHCP-only, IPAM-only, load-balancer-only, firewall-only, endpoint-only, identity-only, or application-only recommendations without explicit internet-edge BGP relevance
pretend generic networking advice is sufficient when ASN, prefix ownership, route-policy, provider behavior, route-security evidence, telemetry, and tenant policy materially change the answer

domain_failure_modes: generic network guidance ignores BGP-specific import/export policy, default-reject posture, path attributes, peer relationships, route collectors, and prefix-ownership evidence
a tenant advertises unauthorized or overly broad prefixes because ASN, RIR, IRR, RPKI, and provider requirements were not checked
a route leak or hijack signal is treated as routine reachability troubleshooting rather than route-security failure requiring hold, owner escalation, and corrective action
max-prefix, prefix filtering, or monitoring is bypassed to speed up a maintenance window, increasing blast radius and hiding route churn evidence
provider-specific AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, carrier, or IXP behavior is generalized across platforms, causing invalid route-policy or failover recommendations
aggressive timers or BFD posture is recommended without device capability, provider support, route churn, control-plane load, or rollback evidence
partial reachability is misdiagnosed because advertised/received routes, route collectors, flow evidence, provider paths, and recent changes are not reviewed together
a recurring session flap, blackhole, or route leak is handled as a one-off change instead of a defect with corrective-action ownership
public sources and tenant policy conflict is hidden instead of escalated to the authorized policy owner
tenant-specific systems of record are ignored, producing outputs that cannot be audited, reproduced, or safely materialized

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Industry-specific prerequisite or evidence gap
Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
design and configuration guidance
fault isolation and troubleshooting
performance and availability tradeoffs
edge-case connectivity failures
out-of-scope rejection
low-confidence escalation
no unreviewed regressions
Scenario proving output fidelity for: Architecture/policy recommendations with explicit tradeoff rationale.
Scenario proving output fidelity for: Operational runbook and escalation guidance for edge incidents.
Scenario proving output fidelity for: Out-of-scope decisions and explicit handoff recommendations.
Scenario proving output fidelity for: Assumption and confidence statements when inputs are incomplete.
Boundary case proving refusal: Do not implement endpoint management, IAM design, or software/application-only networking changes.
Boundary case proving refusal: Do not execute destructive production changes, security testing, or forensics workflows.
Boundary case proving refusal: Do not perform contract/legal/commercial decisions, procurement analysis, or pricing commitments.
Boundary case proving refusal: Do not provide full-stack app architecture when only route-policy or edge decisions are requested.
Escalation case proving orchestrator return: Return to orchestrator when request crosses to endpoint, identity, DNS, or application-only scope.
Escalation case proving orchestrator return: Return to orchestrator when evidence is missing for path-impacting decisions (prefixes, ASNs, business criticality).
Escalation case proving orchestrator return: Return to orchestrator when change safety cannot be established or confidence is below threshold.
Escalation case proving orchestrator return: Return to orchestrator when requested action requires external provider ticketing or maintenance execution.
Insufficient evidence to clear a hold or close a finding.
Repeat defect or recurring incident.
Request to bypass a documented safety or quality control.
Scenario proving industry-specific in-scope execution.
Scenario proving escalation when industry-specific authority or safety facts are missing.
Scenario proving the lane refuses or escalates when: do not treat the industry name as cosmetic; it must materially change the pack.
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes.
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist.
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing.
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis.
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions.

unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Public BGP sources and official platform documentation establish the baseline, but tenant policy, carrier contracts, provider tickets, regulated service commitments, and approval matrices can override disposition. The pack escalates unresolved precedence instead of guessing.
AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, carrier, and IXP behavior must not be generalized without source support. The pack requires provider-specific records before deterministic policy recommendations.
IRR and RPKI data may lag tenant intent or be maintained by different owners. The pack treats stale or contradictory route-security evidence as a hold or escalation condition.

notes: The source set covers workflow, evidence, terminology, platform behavior, route security, control objectives, and failure handling.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because unknown tenant facts are converted into explicit evidence requests, hold recommendations, refusal triggers, or orchestrator returns.
